text:
POST 6  Motive check: Did he gain anything?_x000D_
_x000D_
Power, wealth, status?_x000D_
False prophets seek:_x000D_
  Luxury_x000D_
  Control_x000D_
  Dynasties_x000D_
_x000D_
Muhammad :_x000D_
  Lived in a mud-brick room_x000D_
  Often slept on a straw mat_x000D_
  Died with his armour pawned for food_x000D_
  Left no inheritance_x000D_
_x000D_
When offered kingship and wealth, he refused._x000D_
Even after ruling Arabia, his lifestyle did not change._x000D_
Reminder:_x000D_
People fake religion to gain the world  Muhammad  abandoned the world when it was offered to him._x000D_
_x000D_
The claim that Muhammad  sought worldly gainwealth, power, or prestigeis contradicted by well-attested historical and material evidence from his life._x000D_
_x000D_
Despite ruling Madinah and having access to resources unavailable to him earlier in Makkah, the Prophet  continued to live in extreme material simplicity. His dwelling beside the Prophets Mosque was austere, especially when contrasted with the luxury of contemporary Byzantine and Sasanian rulers. This physical reality remains a tangible historical testimony._x000D_
_x000D_
Multiple authentic reports establish that his lifestyle was one of consistent moderation and self-denial:_x000D_
_x000D_
  Chronic material scarcity: ishah reported that the Prophets household in Madinah did not eat their fill for three consecutive nights until his death (Bukhr 6089; Muslim 2970). Even at the time of his passing, there was nothing edible in his home except a small amount of barley (Bukhari 6086, Muslim 5281)._x000D_
_x000D_
  Voluntary asceticism: This deprivation was not forced by weakness or persecution; it continued unchanged even when wealth and authority were available to him._x000D_
_x000D_
  Aversion to wealth at death: During his final illness, the Prophet  insisted that a small amount of gold in his possession be given away in charity immediately, expressing fear of meeting God with any worldly attachment (reported by Amad; authenticated by al-Haytham)._x000D_
_x000D_
  Eyewitness testimony of companions: Umar ibn al-Khab described finding the Prophet  sleeping on a rough mat that left marks on his body, with only a small measure of barley and a few leaves as provisions. When Umar wept at this contrast with imperial luxury, the Prophet replied: Are you not pleased that they have the world, and we have the Hereafter? (Ibn Mjah; authenticated by al-kim)._x000D_
_x000D_
  No accumulation or inheritance: He died with his armour held as collateral by a Jewish resident of Madinah for barley (Bukhr 2759). He explicitly declared that prophets do not leave inheritance; whatever they leave is charity (Bukhr 6346; Muslim 1758). Consequently, neither his wives nor his daughter inherited wealth from him._x000D_
_x000D_
This patternliving with the bare minimum, dying with the bare minimum, and leaving ones family with the bare minimumis fundamentally inconsistent with the behaviour of a charlatan motivated by self-interest, prestige, or material gain._x000D_
_x000D_
This conclusion is not confined to Muslim scholarship. The New Catholic Encyclopedia states:_x000D_
A case has been made that mere economic gain was the inspiration for Mohammeds religious revolution. That case does not suit the facts as they are known. (The New Catholic Encyclopedia 2003, second edition, vol 10, p52. art. Muhammad.)_x000D_
_x000D_
Taken collectively, the evidence strongly indicates that Muhammad  did not possess, nor act upon, any worldly interest in his claim to Prophethood.

text:
POST 5:_x000D_
_x000D_
Someone assessing Muhammads   claim to prophethood faces a stark set of possibilities: either accept that he was truthful and fully trustworthy in conveying what he believed was revealed to him; and that his role as a messenger between humanity and its Creator was genuine; or conclude that he knowingly fabricated his message and orchestrated a sustained deception against those who followed him. In this framing, a claimant to prophethood would be either exceptionally truthful, deliberately fraudulent, or sincerely mistaken._x000D_
_x000D_
If Muhammads  honesty is rejected, the alternative is to portray him as an unparalleled impostor: inventing portions of the Quran in response to unfolding events, staging words and actions to mimic revelation, and maintaining an elaborate performance of devotion and sincerity. This would also imply consistent deception in private and public life, such as toward his closest Companions, in everyday dealings, and even within his household, by presenting an appearance of integrity while concealing calculated dishonesty._x000D_
_x000D_
Even those who denied his message struggled, deep down, to regard him as a liar; their refusal was rooted instead in resistance to his call to monotheism and to leaving the religion of their ancestors, not in doubts about his personal integrity. The Quran reflects this distinction: We certainly know that what they say grieves you It is not you they accuse of lying, but it is Allahs signs that the wrongdoers deny (Quran 6:33). Accordingly, their stance was less an accusation of falsehood than a rejection of his prophethood driven by obstinacy._x000D_
_x000D_
               _x000D_
_x000D_
Paul Casanova, a French Orientalist states: _x000D_
'Before entering into the heart of the question, I wish to state at once that I reject a priori any theory tending to suspect Mohammeds sincerity. No doubt one may be led to believe that, in his later years, the necessities of politics led him to present his personal ideas as divine revelations, so as to secure better obedience. But that must not cast doubt on his sincerity; a modern psychologist may see in it auto-suggestion, and it is contrary to every scientific mind to assert, without proofs, that there was imposture and calculation. The whole history of the Arab Prophet proves that his character was positive, serious, and loyal. Even when his absolute power was acknowledged, he knew how to listen to advice, admit his faults, and correct them'_x000D_
(Casanova, P. (1911). Mohammed et la fin du monde: tude critique sur lIslam primitif. Paris: Geuthner. P5)_x000D_
_x000D_
When Muhammad  was instructed to summon his close kin to Islam, he chose a public, familiar setting and addressed the Quraysh clans from Mount af, calling them by name. He then tested the credibility they already attributed to him: If I told you that horsemen were in the valley preparing to attack you, would you believe me? They replied that they would, because they had only ever known him to be truthful. (a al-Bukhr, hadith 4523)._x000D_
_x000D_
He met lifes hardships with a calm spirit and turned away from temptation with unwavering resolve. He deliberately chose a demanding road marked by isolation and painful trials, driven by the task of conveying what he believed to be the truth: calling people away from inherited idolatry and moral corruption, and gathering them around virtue and piety._x000D_
How can that portrait be made consistent with the opposing claim sometimes advanced by criticsthat he was a liar, animated by deception and emptied of genuine goodness? The two pictures point in opposite directions; holding them together as equally true creates a contradiction that needs explaining rather than assertion._x000D_
_x000D_
It is strongly corroborative of Mahomets sincerity that the earliest converts to Islam were not only of upright character, but his own bosom friends and people of_x000D_
his household; who, intimately acquainted with his private life, could not fail otherwise to have detected those discrepancies which ever more or less exist between the professions of the hypocritical deceiver abroad and his actions at home. (Muir, W. (1877). Life of Mahomet: From Original Sources. London:Smith, Elder, and Co. p60)_x000D_
_x000D_
Heracliusdescribed as perceptive and shrewdquestioned Ab Sufyn while Ab Sufyn was still a non-believer and openly hostile to Muhammad , and asked whether Muhammad  had ever been accused of lying. Ab Sufyn replied that he had not, so Heraclius reasoned that someone who would not lie to people would be unlikely to fabricate a lie about God. (Reported in a al-Bukhr, hadith 4278, and a Muslim, hadith 1773.)_x000D_
_x000D_

-He lived modestly despite absolute authority_x000D_
-He died without wealth, dynasty, or inheritance_x000D_
_x000D_

text:
POST 4  Moral character_x000D_
_x000D_
Moral uprightness as evidence_x000D_
_x000D_
Before prophethood, Muhammad  was universally known as:_x000D_
al-diq al-Amn  The Truthful, The Trustworthy_x000D_
_x000D_
This reputation was not given by followers _x000D_
It was acknowledged by his enemies._x000D_
_x000D_
Key question:_x000D_
Would divine guidance be entrusted to someone morally corrupt?_x000D_
Across every dimension:_x000D_
Honesty_x000D_
Mercy_x000D_
Patience_x000D_
Humility_x000D_
Self-restraint_x000D_
_x000D_
Muhammad  exhibited consistent moral excellence, privately and publicly._x000D_
_x000D_
Even his closest attendant (Anas ibn Mlik) reported:_x000D_
By Allah, I served him for seven or nine yearsI do not know that he said for something I did not do: Why did you not do such and such? (Muslim 2310)_x000D_
_x000D_
When the feet of the Prophet were swollen from_x000D_
standing in worship, it was said to him, O Messenger of Allah, Allah has forgiven you all your past sins. He replied, So should I not be a thankful servant? (Bukhari 4557, Muslim 2819,  wording from Ahmad 18114)_x000D_
_x000D_
His companion Anas reports: ''There was no person more beloved to them than the Messenger of Allah. When they saw him, they would not stand, as they knew he disliked it (Tirmidh i 335)_x000D_
_x000D_
When Ab Hurayrah requested that he place a sheet_x000D_
over the mat that left a mark on the Prophets back, he replied, And what connection do I have with the world? In relation to this world, I am just like a traveller who took shade under a tree, and then he got up and left.'  (Tirmidh 2377)_x000D_
_x000D_
When Makkah was conquered, Ab Bakr carried_x000D_
his own father so that he would give his pledge to the Messenger of God. He said, Why could you not leave the old man at his house, so that we would be the ones to visit him? (Amad 27001, Ibn ibbn 7208, and kim 4363)_x000D_
_x000D_
The Prophet () said, "When I start the prayer I intend to prolong it, but on hearing the cries of a child, I cut short the prayer because I know that the cries of the child will incite its mother's passions." (Bukhr 709); and Muslim 192)._x000D_
_x000D_
Abdullh ibn Masd said, We were with the Messenger of Allah on a journey. We saw a desert lark with two chicks. We took the chicks. The lark came and apped her wings furiously. When the Messenger came, he said, Who has grieved this bird over its chicks? Return the chicks to her.  (Ab Dwd, 5268)_x000D_
_x000D_
Frauds do not maintain moral consistency across decades, hardship, power, and intimacy.

text:
POST 2  How prophethood is tested_x000D_
_x000D_
Extraordinary claims require transparent evaluation._x000D_
_x000D_
Prophethood is not about charisma or popularity. It is about truthful transmission of revelation._x000D_
_x000D_
Any claim to prophethood must satisfy at least these two conditions:_x000D_
1.  Truthfulness of the claimant_x000D_
2.  Irrefutable evidence for the claim_x000D_
_x000D_
We therefore ask:_x000D_
  Was Muhammad  known to lie?_x000D_
  Did he provide demonstrable evidence for his claims?_x000D_
  Did his life align with what he preached?_x000D_
_x000D_
Crucially, neutral history leaves us only three options:_x000D_
  Muhammad  was profoundly honest (i.e. a genuine prophet of God)_x000D_
  Or he was sincerely deluded_x000D_
  Or he was the greatest deceiver in human history_x000D_
_x000D_
There is no other reasonable position. The idea that he is a legendary figure shaped by later followers, is not worth entertaining.

text:
How do we critically test a claim to Prophethood?_x000D_
_x000D_
Series Introduction_x000D_
Many people ask:Why should anyone believe Muhammad  was a true prophet of God?_x000D_
_x000D_
This series does not begin with blind faith. It begins withcritical evaluation._x000D_
_x000D_
Any serious assessment of prophethood must answer three preliminary questions:_x000D_
_x000D_
Does God exist?_x000D_
Does God communicate with humanity?_x000D_
Are miracles and revelation logically possible?_x000D_
_x000D_
If the answer to these is yes, then the question becomes empirical and historical:_x000D_
_x000D_
Did Muhammad  truthfully convey a message from God, or did he fabricate it?_x000D_
_x000D_
In this series, we will examine his claim usingpublic evidence, not theological presuppositions._x000D_
_x000D_
What this series will cover:_x000D_
Each post will address one critical criterion used by historians and philosophers when evaluating prophetic claims:_x000D_
-Preservation of Muhammads  life and message_x000D_
-His moral character before and after prophethood_x000D_
-His honesty and reputation among allies and enemies_x000D_
-Psychological sincerity vs deception or delusion_x000D_
-Worldly motives  power, wealth, status?_x000D_
-The Quran as a public, testable miracle_x000D_
-Historical miracles and mass testimony_x000D_
-Addressing common objections_x000D_
_x000D_
Outcome:_x000D_
Either Muhammad  was:_x000D_
_x000D_
-A deliberate fraud_x000D_
-A sincerely deluded man_x000D_
-Or a genuine prophet of God_x000D_
_x000D_
Onlyoneof these explanations survives critical scrutiny.

text:
"The great scholar, al-Imm 'Abdur Rahmn as-Sa'd, Allah have mercy upon him, mentions the benefits of mutual consultation (al-mushwarah):

1. Implementing the commandments of Allah, and following a path that Allah loves, as Allah has described the believers as people of mutual consultation. The Messenger, sallallhu 'alyhi wa sallam would consult his companions.

2. It brings about cohesion amongst the believers.

3. Ideas are clarified and intellects are nurtured.

4. It may be that the correct path is a collobarotation of 2 or 3 opinions.
Correctness in an affair is not known until it is compared with the incorrect position by a sound intellect. 

5. Mutual consultation nurtures love between the believers. 

Majmu'ah of the Shaykh v.26 p.120"

text:
"Follow the Dall, Not the Muft - 'Allmah Muqbil bin Hdi, Allah have mercy on him_x000D_
_x000D_
al-Allmah Muqbil bin Had al-Wdi, Allah have mercy on him, was asked regarding the befitting stance of a person on a fatwa from a single scholar; specifically in matters of disagreement. Should it suffice him or should he ask as many scholars as possible, and then follow a fatwa from their fatwa? Is this acceptable and is it negligent to ask only a single scholar? _x000D_
_x000D_
His answer:_x000D_
_x000D_
What I advise the brother with, if he is a student of knowledge, is that he should research in the books, and should diligently attempt to find the truth himself from the books of the people of knowledge, and the affair is easy, Allah willing. And if this is not easy, then what matters is the ease of the heart. So if the heart is assured by asking the first scholar [whose answers come] with proof [then this is acceptable]. _x000D_
_x000D_
You must ask for proof, because Allah says, Follow what has been revealed to you from your Lord and do not follow other than Him any awliy. Little do you remember.  [Surat al-Arf: 3]. If he brings proof from the book of Allah and from the sunnah of the Messenger of Allah, sallallhu alaihi wa sallam, and you have understood the proof, then suffice yourself with this, or then you can ask another. And be sure that the proof is not abrogated, or that it is not a proof for what it is being used for, as it may be that the muft takes to a madh-hab, and is giving a fatwa which agrees with his madh-hab._x000D_
_x000D_
Ijbatus-Sil, p.638_x000D_
Translation: Abu Hafsah Kashiff Khan"

text:
" A night porter encouraged a 12-year old-girl to use a sex toy on herself and even asked her to come and stay in a hotel room with him while on holiday in Cornwall so he could rape her_x000D_
_x000D_
Jonathan Pound started engaging in communication with a child when he joined the Kik messaging app which is known to be targeted at and used by children and teenagers._x000D_
_x000D_
He contacted a girl aged 12 and soon the conversation took a sexualised turn in nature._x000D_
_x000D_
A sentencing hearing at Truro Crown Court yesterday (Monday, April 27) heard how the conversation - even when Pound knew perfectly well that the girl was 12 years old - went from flirtatious to disgusting._x000D_
_x000D_
In 15 exchanges over five weeks, Pound, 44, from Station Road in Grampound Road, between Truro and St Austell, asked the girl if she was into older men, if she had a nice body and even asked her to send him pictures of her breasts and genitals._x000D_
_x000D_
The court heard that on one occasion he asked her to go in the house, locate a sex toy and insert it into her vagina and then he sent her a picture of his penis._x000D_
_x000D_
It was when he tried to encourage the girl to meet up at a hotel room he would book and asked her to come alone so staff would not bat an eyelid but would think she was his own daughter, that Devon and Cornwall Police, who had run the fake-girl account as a paedo-hunting decoy, pounced._x000D_
_x000D_
Pound was arrested and charged with one count of inciting a girl under 13 to engage in sexual activity and another of engaging in sexual communication with a child in connection with the incident in February/March 2024._x000D_
_x000D_
The 44-year-old dad of two young children later pleaded guilty to the charges._x000D_
_x000D_
His barrister said the incident happened two years ago and had been a one-off and Pound had not offended either before or since._x000D_
_x000D_
She said that Pound denied having any sexual interest in children but admitted that what he did was wrong, adding that he had been lonely at the time with no-one to speak to about his mental health issues._x000D_
_x000D_
His Honour Judge Simon Carr said Pound had intended to use Kik because it is targeted at and used by children and teens. _x000D_
_x000D_
He said Pound's conversation with the fake child were entirely sexualised and abhorrent in nature._x000D_
_x000D_
Judge Carr said Pound's offences were so serious an immediate custodial sentence was unavoidable._x000D_
_x000D_
He sentenced Pound to three years in prison. He will serve half before he is eligible to be released on licence. _x000D_
_x000D_
He will also be on the sex offenders' register for life and a sexual harm prevention order restricting his access to the internet was imposed for 10 years. _x000D_
_x000D_
Source UK Database."
label: label_Irrelevant

text:
" THEY CALL IT CEASEFIRE WHILE GAZA STARVES, OCCUPIED PALESTINE BLEEDS, AND THE WORLD LOOKS AWAY | APRIL 27_x000D_
_x000D_
 Aid / Crossings_x000D_
The Gaza Crossing and Border Authority reported 214 trucks entered through Kerem Abu Salem: 134 commercial trucks, 12 aid trucks and 12 fuel trucks. For over 2 million people, this is not relief. It is controlled deprivation. They open the crossing when they choose, close it when they choose, then claim aid is flowing._x000D_
_x000D_
 Water Crisis_x000D_
Gazas water collapse is catastrophic. Families are surviving on less than 10 litres per person daily, with some areas down to 2 litres. Before the war, access was around 80 litres. Water production has fallen from around 100,000 cubic metres per day to 35,000. Dirty water, sewage overflow, disease, insects and rats are now part of survival._x000D_
_x000D_
 Ceasefire Violations_x000D_
Palestinian officials have cited 2,400+ serious israeli violations, including 921 shootings, 1,109 bombardments and attacks, 273 demolitions and 97 incursions. They call it a ceasefire. They keep firing._x000D_
_x000D_
 Imad Miqdad_x000D_
Imad Miqdad was killed after israeli war criminal drones targeted his solar powered phone charging station in Sheikh Radwan. This was one of the few places civilians, journalists and medics could charge phones and stay connected. Destroy hospitals. Destroy water. Destroy roads. Destroy communication._x000D_
_x000D_
 Occupied Palestine / East Jerusalem_x000D_
Occupied Palestine is being strangled in parallel. Since 7 October 2023, OCHA figures cited by UNRWA recorded 1,079 Palestinians killed in occupied Palestine, including East Jerusalem, at least 235 of them children, by 30 March 2026. Save the Children reported 685 Palestinian children displaced by settler violence in the first three months of 2026 alone. In al-Mughayyir near Ramallah, 14 year old Aws al-Naasan and 32 year old Jihad Abu Naim were killed during a settler attack near a school. This is raids, shootings, demolitions, illegal outposts, land seizures, settler terror and forced displacement working together._x000D_
_x000D_
 Dr Hussam Abu Safiya_x000D_
Dr Hussam Abu Safiya, a Palestinian doctor from Gaza, remains in israeli detention without charge according to campaigners and rights advocates. April 28 is the feared key date being raised for his case. He treated the wounded after losing his own son. Then he was taken._x000D_
_x000D_
 Knesset Death Penalty Bill_x000D_
The israeli Knesset passed a death penalty bill targeting Palestinians, advancing execution measures through occupation courts. So while Gaza is starved and bombed, Palestinian prisoners are threatened with execution through the same system that jails doctors without justice._x000D_
_x000D_
 Core Closing Line_x000D_
They jail the doctors, threaten the prisoners, starve the civilians, cut the water, throttle the aid, kill the children, seize the land, build the settlements, then ask the world to call it democracy."

" A man who exposed himself to multiple women on a London train, breaching a Sexual Harm Prevention Order (SHPO) handed to him less than two weeks prior has been jailed, following a British Transport Police (BTP) investigation._x000D_
_x000D_
Ayman Yanoussi, 21, and of St Johns Road in Newham, was sentenced at Inner London Crown Court on Thursday 16 April to 14 months imprisonment having previously pleaded guilty to two counts of exposure, breaching his SHPO, and possessing cannabis._x000D_
_x000D_
The court heard how on 28 December 2025, at 11.20am, Yanoussi was at Upminster Bridge Underground station sat on a bench on the platform._x000D_
_x000D_
A woman who walked up the platform noticed that Yanoussi was sat with his trousers by his ankles while touching himself inappropriately._x000D_
_x000D_
She took out her phone to record what he was doing to pass on to police, but he quickly pulled his trousers up and hid behind a pillar. He then boarded a District Line train heading towards Wimbledon._x000D_
_x000D_
Not long after he boarded the train, Yanoussi sat next to another woman and pulled his trousers down. After that woman moved away, Yanoussi sat opposite another woman, again exposing himself to her before she moved away._x000D_
_x000D_
Yanoussi repeated this behaviour a third time, sitting next to another woman and exposing himself as the train approached Elm Park at which point several of the women who Yanoussi had targeted left the train._x000D_
_x000D_
Yanoussi continued to expose himself on different trains across London while occasionally touching himself inappropriately until 12.51pm when a woman reported his behaviour to a member of staff._x000D_
_x000D_
The train Yanoussi was on was held at Hammersmith Underground station, and Yanoussi quickly fled the station._x000D_
_x000D_
A BTP officer was dispatched to the station and found Yanoussi waiting at a bus stop where he was promptly arrested for exposure. _x000D_
_x000D_
After being detained, it emerged that Yanoussi had been handed a Sexual Harm Prevention Order less than two weeks earlier after he was sentenced to nine months' imprisonment, suspended for 18 months, having pleaded guilty to three counts of Upskirting that occurred in April 2024._x000D_
_x000D_
Source UK Database."

text:
" A primary school teaching assistant groomed a 10-year-old child into sending sexual videos on Snapchat. _x000D_
_x000D_
Charlie Territt, who worked at Streatham Wells Primary School, was sentenced to seven years in prison on Thursday (April 23) by a judge who described him as having a perverted fixation with the victim. _x000D_
_x000D_
Prosecutor Bhavin Patel said that throughout 2024, Territt engaged in late-night conversations with a vulnerable child on Snapchat. _x000D_
_x000D_
The 32-year-old sent sexual videos of himself and incited the child to send similar videos in return. _x000D_
_x000D_
Noticing a change in behaviour, the victim's elder sibling inspected the Snapchat account and discovered Territts vile messages. _x000D_
_x000D_
The abuse was reported to the police and on January 3 last year, police attended Territts home in South Bermondsey where they seized several phones and sex toys. _x000D_
_x000D_
Mr Patel said: "Whilst police were inside his home, a car left the property. The driver and occupant were the defendants brother and cousin. The car was stopped and two more mobile phones were seized. _x000D_
_x000D_
Territt denied the abuse in interview and continued to do so after being charged, until an analysis of his phone revealed a sexual video of the victim as well as indecent images of three other children. _x000D_
_x000D_
Facing incontrovertible evidence, according to Judge Silas Reid, Territt then pleaded guilty to:_x000D_
_x000D_
- Causing or inciting a child to engage in sexual activity, _x000D_
_x000D_
- Causing a child to watch a sexual act, _x000D_
_x000D_
- Sexual communication with a child  _x000D_
_x000D_
- Making indecent photographs of children aged between 10 and 16. _x000D_
_x000D_
However, the defence said his client denied being a paedophile and that it was an attraction to this child as opposed to a wider interest. _x000D_
_x000D_

text:
" Sorry folks, another grim one._x000D_
_x000D_
 A diaper fetishist" with a grotesque fixation on rooting through bins for used nappies has been jailed for a total of 2.5 years for doing the same thing again._x000D_
_x000D_
Abbie Taylor had been banned from going near bins but was seen by two different people climbing in and searching through their waste, Newcastle Crown Court heard._x000D_
_x000D_
Taylor, who identifies as a woman but previously committed offences under the name Martin Tarling and was being held on remand at a male prison, was given a suspended sentence in 2025 for targeting nurseries in South Tyneside._x000D_
_x000D_
The 47-year-old from South Shields, whose criminal record had 99 offences on it, has admitted breaching a criminal behaviour order and was jailed for two and a half years._x000D_
_x000D_
A three-year order was made in 2023 prohibiting Taylor from attending any nurseries, removing items from bins or wearing incontinence aids over clothing in public, the prosecutor said._x000D_
_x000D_
Taylor was originally given a suspended two years sentence in August 2025 for breaching the 2023 order by committing offences at several nurseries which included repeatedly dumping soiled nappies near nurseries and raking through the bins semi-naked._x000D_
_x000D_
In December 2025, a woman who lived with her husband, who needed to use adult nappies, found several of his soiled garments scattered around their garden, the court heard._x000D_
_x000D_
She believed they might have been blown out by the wind, but later that day heard someone moving her bins and looked out to see Taylor rummaging, the court heard._x000D_
_x000D_
The woman banged on the window and shouted, causing Taylor to flee semi-dressed, the court heard._x000D_
_x000D_
The following day, the woman saw Taylor going through her bin again, with the defendant leaving with a bag of rubbish, the court heard._x000D_
_x000D_
Shortly afterward in December another woman saw Taylor standing in her bin and searching, the prosecutor said._x000D_
_x000D_
A previous court hearing was told Taylor was a "diaper fetishist" whose Facebook profile described the defendant as an "adult baby diaper lover"._x000D_
_x000D_
But in mitigation, a psychiatrist had concluded Taylor 's actions were not sexually motivated, which was accepted by the judge._x000D_
_x000D_
The judge said Taylor had a "grotesque obsession" with "rooting around in bins near care homes or nurseries to retrieve soiled nappies or incontinence wear"._x000D_
_x000D_
"Your conduct is highly anti-social and extremely distressing to people who are affected by it," the judge told Taylor._x000D_
_x000D_
Taylor was jailed for 16 months for breaching the 2023 court order and 14 months consecutive for breaching the suspended sentence imposed in August 2025._x000D_
_x000D_
Source True Crime Online."

"NEW: The confession of the perpetrator of the Tadamon Massacre, Amjad Yousesf:_x000D_
_x000D_
 The individual appearing in the viral footage known as the Tadamon massacre is me, Amjad Youssef. These events date back to the early years of the Syrian revolution. _x000D_
_x000D_
 At the time, I was accompanied by Najib al-Halabi and a photographer whose name I cannot recall. We rounded up 40 individuals at random, under the pretext that they were 'terrorists'._x000D_
_x000D_
 We took them to a pre-dug pit, where I began executing them, as shown in the video. In some instances, I waited for the victim to fall into the pit before firing; in others, I shot them before they fell. _x000D_
_x000D_
 Intermittently, I would call on Najib al-Halabi to take his turn in the killing, until we had executed everyone present._x000D_
_x000D_
 We had previously placed car tires at the bottom of the pit. After the executions, we piled more tires over the bodies and set them on fire to prevent the scent of decomposition from spreading throughout the area. We then filled the pit. _x000D_
_x000D_
 I received no military orders to carry out this massacre, it was my own personal decision. _x000D_
_x000D_
 I cannot explain why there were exactly 40 victims, the selection was arbitrary, dictated solely by the circumstances at the time._x000D_
_x000D_
Source: Usman A Khan and translation by Syrian journalist Qusay Noor"
